 {"id":"bd-lff4p.8","title":"Spec: Correctness strategy for terminal engines (invariants, fuzz, golden traces)","description":"Goal
- Specify correctness guarantees strong enough to justify replacing BOTH Crossterm and xterm.js.

Correctness Model
- Treat the terminal engine as a deterministic state machine driven only by explicit inputs:
  - output byte streams (VT/ANSI) and/or patches
  - input events (keyboard/mouse/touch/IME)
  - resize events
  - ticks/time (explicitly injected; never implicit global time)

Must Include
- Formal-ish invariants (written down, then enforced):
  - parser state machine safety (no panic, bounded memory growth, valid transitions)
  - grid mutations (cursor bounds, width invariants, no invalid placeholder cells)
  - scrollback correctness (capacity, wrap/reflow policy, viewport invariants)
  - selection semantics (spans scrollback+viewport; stable under resize where specified)
  - hyperlink semantics (OSC 8 lifetime + mapping)
- Proptest strategy
  - generators for byte streams, events, and resize storms
  - shrinking strategy that yields minimal repros
- Fuzzing strategy
  - entrypoints + corpora + crash triage protocol
  - every crash becomes a regression fixture
- Golden artifacts
  - conformance fixtures (byte stream -> expected grid snapshots)
  - golden trace corpus (event log -> expected checksums)
  - renderer goldens (framebuffer/patch checksums for key scenes)
- Differential testing (high-value)
  - compare FrankenTerm state against reference emulators for the same byte streams

E2E & Logging Requirements
- Native: `tests/e2e/scripts/*` must remain green and emit detailed JSONL.
- Web: a trace runner must replay the same golden traces and verify checksums.
- JSONL must conform to `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` (extend schema if needed, never break silently).
- Every run logs: run_id, build_id/git_sha, seed, size, caps/profile, perf counters, and checksums.

Acceptance Criteria
- A concrete test matrix mapping each user-facing feature to unit/property/fuzz/e2e coverage.
- CI hooks exist for: wasm build checks, proptest, fuzz smoke, trace replay, and checksum gates.
- A "never regress" list exists (copy/paste, selection, OSC8, resize, IME/composition, focus/mouse modes) with golden artifacts.
","notes":"Drafted docs/spec/frankenterm-correctness.md (invariants/proptest/fuzz/goldens + coverage matrix + CI hook checklist) and linked it from README Key Docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:04.113466063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:30.644299453Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:30.644277773Z","close_reason":"Completed: added docs/spec/frankenterm-correctness.md + README link","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:04.113466063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.9","title":"ADR: terminal backend strategy (replace Crossterm; enable WASM; unify interfaces)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the backend architecture that removes Crossterm, supports WASM, and keeps ftui APIs clean.

Questions to Answer
- What is the core backend trait surface?
- How do we handle feature toggles (mouse/paste/focus/kitty) without leaking platform details?
- How do we represent time, sleep/ticks, and async tasks in WASM (no threads)?
- Migration plan that avoids long-lived compatibility shims.

Acceptance Criteria
- A crisp, implementable interface + crate map.
- A staged migration plan with "delete old code" checkpoints (file deletion rule applies).
","notes":"Drafted ADR in docs/adr/ADR-008-terminal-backend-strategy.md (Status: PROPOSED). Covers: backend boundary at runtime (EventSource+Presenter+Clock/Executor), new crates (ftui-backend/ftui-tty/ftui-web), isolating Crossterm during migration, wasm no-threads effect execution, staged migration with delete checkpoints + explicit-permission note for deletions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:10.491519002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:56.057130527Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:56.057111191Z","close_reason":"Completed: ADR drafted in docs/adr/ADR-008-terminal-backend-strategy.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:10.491519002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-lrito","title":"Update all dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"## Dependency Updates Required

Closed Dependabot PRs:
- actions/setup-python 5 -> 6 (CI: Python setup action)
- codecov/codecov-action 4 -> 5 (CI: coverage upload)
- nix 0.29.0 -> 0.31.1 (MODERATE: Unix API bindings, minor version but nix crate often has breaking changes)

## Steps
1. Run cargo update
2. Review nix 0.29 -> 0.31 changelog for breaking changes (nix crate frequently removes deprecated APIs)
3. Fix any compilation errors
4. Run: cargo check --all-targets && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
5. Run: cargo test
6. Update GH Actions versions in .github/workflows/*.yml

## Testing
- cargo test must pass
- cargo clippy must pass with -D warnings
- Verify terminal handling still works (nix crate provides ioctl, termios, etc.)
- CI must pass on push","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayCat","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:20:41.628925003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:40:28.117687298Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lrito","depends_on_id":"bd-ll6nr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:34:01.319515916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1135,"issue_id":"bd-lrito","author":"SunnyMarsh","text":"Reopened: CI pins still on setup-python@v5 and codecov-action@v4; need bump + re-run gates","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:40:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mevj","title":"Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests","description":"# Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests

## Background
Part of Headless Rendering Mode (bd-3m7u). Tests ensure headless mode works for CI testing.

## Unit Tests
- headless_term_processes_ansi_correctly: CSI sequences update screen state
- cursor_movement_works: CSI H, CSI A/B/C/D move cursor correctly
- style_state_tracked: SGR sequences update style
- screen_text_returns_content: screen_text() matches expected output
- assert_matches_works: Assertion helper functions correctly

## Integration Tests
- snapshot_test_workflow: Full snapshot test with HeadlessTerm
- complex_layout_renders_correctly: Nested widgets render as expected
- style_codes_produce_correct_output: Colors and attributes work

## Property Tests
- any_ansi_sequence_processed_safely: Malformed sequences dont crash
- cursor_stays_in_bounds: Cursor never goes outside screen

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests verify real CI workflow
- [ ] Headless mode matches real terminal behavior
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:13.872368352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978575591Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978505329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mevj","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
